user_query:
I need a reproducible, containerized export setup for my Madam Tulip cover comps and a polished client email. Please do the following using the provided inputs under input/:

1) Inspect the SVGs to understand what they need:
- Look at input/assets/svgs/. Identify the font families referenced by the SVGs and list them in your report (see outputs below).

2) Create a Docker-based export environment with Inkscape:
- Create docker/Dockerfile that builds from Ubuntu 22.04 LTS and installs Inkscape via apt. Keep the image minimal but usable for batch exports.
- Create docker-compose.yml at the project root that builds the above image as a service named cover-proofs and mounts:
  - ./input/assets/svgs to /work/in (read-only is fine), and
  - ./out to /work/out.
  Set working_dir to /work.

3) Add reproducible batch export commands:
- Create a Makefile at the project root with these targets:
  - build: builds the Docker image via docker compose.
  - png: exports every SVG from input/assets/svgs to 300 DPI PNGs under out/proofs/png/, preserving base filenames.
  - pdf: exports every SVG from input/assets/svgs to PDFs under out/proofs/pdf/, preserving base filenames.
  - all: runs png and pdf.
  - clean: removes everything under out/proofs/.
- Use the official Inkscape command-line options (Inkscape 1.x syntax) for batch exporting. Ensure 300 DPI for PNG exports. For PDFs, configure the command so that fonts are embedded if supported by the CLI (and note any limitations in the report). Implement the Makefile so it invokes docker compose run --rm cover-proofs with appropriate bind mounts to perform the exports without requiring local Inkscape.

4) Document decisions and sources:
- Create out/REPORT.md with:
  - Fonts found in the provided SVGs (list each family once).
  - A brief explanation of your Dockerfile choices.
  - The exact Inkscape CLI flags you used for both PNG and PDF exports, with a one-line rationale for each.
  - A "Sources consulted" section listing at least two references you found via a search engine: one must be the official Inkscape command-line documentation from the Inkscape project, and the other a reputable source discussing print proof DPI guidance or font embedding in PDFs. Provide the source title and the organization/publisher name. Do not include direct URLs.

5) Rewrite a client email:
- Rewrite input/email_draft.txt into a concise, professional message suitable for a publisher contact. Keep it under 180 words, clearly explaining how to run the export (build, then make all) and what the outputs are. Save it to out/email_polished.txt.

Notes and constraints:
- Do not assume I have Inkscape installed locally; all commands should run via Docker/Compose.
- Do not actually run any exports; just provide the configuration, Makefile, and docs.
- Keep output paths exactly as specified below so I can verify them.

Deliverables (create or overwrite these files):
- docker/Dockerfile
- docker-compose.yml
- Makefile
- out/REPORT.md
- out/email_polished.txt

Inputs you must use:
- input/assets/svgs/madam_tulip_front.svg
- input/assets/svgs/madam_tulip_spine.svg
- input/email_draft.txt All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
